text.text:
There was so much hype around it and I finally visited today. Today was also the only time I would ever visit this bakery.

Service is great from the cashier and barista, no issues there.

But overall the food is just lackluster.

Our order consisted of:
•Alexander Toast: thick toast topped with bacon and cheese. This was good, but nothing extraordinary that would make me order it again. We asked to get it warmed up and it definitely was not warmed, it was still room temperature when we ate it

•Egg Tart: again, good but nothing making me want to return. I did appreciate how it was more of a custard filling so the texture was rich and velvety. But the pastry was soggy.

•Truffle Baguette: the bread was stale and dry, and there was just a brush of the truffle cheese/mixture on top and inside. It was honestly so overpowering we thought there would be SOMETHING to coincide with it to balance it out

•Walnut Cream Bread: again, dry and pretty bland with bitter walnuts and a plain whipped cream inside.

To drink I got the matcha cloud which was fine, plenty of other places with better matcha. The matcha itself was mixed well and they don’t sweeten it which I liked. But the barista said the foam top is sweet but it definitely was not sweet at all. It just tasted like plain foam.

Being the only customers for the first 30-40 minutes after opening, it’s safe to say the hype has all but diminished

text.text: Me and my brother and our friend we’re at an event down the street here in Beverly Hills. When we went for a walk, we ran into Albert who was working in this bakery called High Bake He was awesome very kind and knowledgeable. We came in to grab a few pastries and espresso, the experience was amazing. First of all their pastries were out of this world with such a great selection. The ambience was amazing, super clean spot. Also we had an espresso with our pastry and it was honestly delicious! I absolutely would recommend this place to any and everyone that is in the Beverly Hills area and has a sweet tooth or wants to just stop by and have an espresso. This is definitely not my last time coming here being that I’m always in the area and their products are absolutely amazing and delicious also, they are not too sweet. Definitely will be back to this spot again. Thank you Hi Bake we really appreciate you!

text.text:
Hi Bake in Beverly Hills is a wonderful spot with great taste, good service, and very reasonable prices, especially for the area. Everything we tried was fresh, well made, and beautifully presented.

We ordered the flying fish roe baguette, truffle baguette, and beef sausage potato toast, and each item had its own unique flavor. The baguettes were crispy on the outside and soft inside, with generous toppings and well-balanced seasoning. The beef sausage potato toast was filling, flavorful, and very satisfying.

For drinks and desserts, we tried the California Sunset and the Dubai chocolate egg tart, which were both excellent. The California Sunset was refreshing and nicely balanced, while the Dubai chocolate egg tart was rich, indulgent, and a perfect way to end the meal.

The service was friendly and efficient, and the overall atmosphere was clean and comfortable. Hi Bake is a great place for breakfast, brunch, or a casual bite with high-quality food at a fair price. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in Beverly Hills!

text.text:
⸻

I recently visited Hibake | Asian Fusion Bakery in Beverly Hills, and I was pleasantly surprised! The bakery has a very stylish and cozy interior, and the staff is warm and welcoming. The pastries are quite unique in flavor – not overly sweet, but still very delicious and satisfying. Prices are reasonable, just a little above average, which feels fair considering the quality.

The only thing I would love to see in the future is some gluten-free options, since not everyone can enjoy baked goods with gluten, but they would definitely love to try these creations too. Overall, it’s a wonderful new spot worth visiting!

⸻

text.text: This place seems like a tart specialist. The tarts are perfectly crunchy yet soft, and the sweetness is just right to enjoy with coffee. I highly recommend the Egg Mochi Tart. The Walnut Bread was also a hit with its nice cream-to-walnut ratio. On the other hand, the long cream bread was a bit hit-or-miss with a confusing flavor, so I wouldn't really recommend that one. The Pistachio Latte was a bit underwhelming because it didn't have enough pistachio cream. The flavor was a little vague. I'd definitely recommend the regular Latte or the Cream Top Coffee instead.
